rulings.Case Title: TAMIKO POWELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment Entered On March 18, 2003 And November 18, 2003 Due To Fraud Upon Court Ccp 473(D)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF TAMIKO POWELL'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment Entered On March 18, 2003 And November 18, 2003 Due To Fraud Upon Court CCP 473(D). DENIED. The November 18, 2003 judgment in CCSF's favor is based on the Court's granting of Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings on August 18, 2003, on the basis that Plaintiff's claims were barred by the Statute of Limitations. Plaintiff does not now provide any explanation for how the claim is not barred by the statute of limitations. Nor does Plaintiff explain why the Court should entertain this motion now, 12 years after the entry of judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE RUDNITSKY et al VS. QI YUAN HU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidentiary Preclusion Or Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And Failure To Appear. Memorandum Of Points And Authorities. Declaration Of Lori R. Mayfield, Proposed Order Lodged And Served As A Separate Document.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT QI HU'S Motion For Evidentiary Preclusion Or Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And Failure To Appear. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required on issue of adequacy of notice of moving papers; no opposition received. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW, BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. GRANTED IN PART. On August 1, 2014 a demurrer to Plaitiff's first amended complaint was sustained with leave to amend to plead facts regarding when Defendants' representation of Plaintiff ended. The first amended complaint, and the subsequently filed second amended complaint, both contained only three causes of action. Before a demurrer on the second amended complaint could be heard, Plaintiff filed a third amended complaint, without leave of court, which was struck on October 1, 2014, with leave to amend to file a new third amended complaint, to correct the same errors identified in the Court's August 1, 2014 order. Instead, Plaintiff added several new causes of action. Leave to amend to correct an issue identified by the Court is leave to amend to correct that issue only; it does not serve as leave to amend to add new causes of action except when the new cause of action "directly responds to the court's reason for sustaining the earlier demurrer." (Patrick v. Alacer Corp. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 995, 1005.) The Court's grant of leave to amend was only to plead the date of the end of Defendants' representation; it did not extend to permission to add new causes of action to the complaint. The Court therefore strikes the added fourth and fifth causes of action, for conversion and negligence. The Court does not strike the first, second, and third causes of action, which were properly amended in accordance with the Court's prior orders. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID RAWLINGS ET AL VS. TALK FREE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Staying Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 17, DEFENDANT DOUG COLE, JOI SCHEELE, STAN TEETS, TALK FREE, INC.'S Motion For Protective Order Staying Discovery. Discovery is stayed pending a ruling on Defendants' Demurrers to the First Amended Complaint. If that ruling determines that plaintiffs, or any of them, have standing, then any discovery now pending is deemed to have been served and Defendants shall have thirty days to respond unless mutually agreed otherwise. Nothing in the instant discovery ruling shall be used by either party as evidence of a substantive analysis by this court of the issues to be decided in the demurrers. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. ROBERT MILLER AKA ROBERT G MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. GRANT Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment. Defendant failed to repay monies due under his agreement with Plaintiff. All records and statements show that Defendant applied to Plaintiff for a credit card account, entered into a written credit card account agreement with Plaintiff, and made purchases from this account. There is no evidence presented to this Court indicating that Defendant objected to any billing statement submitted by plaintiff as required under Civil Code section 1747.02 or U.S.C. section 1666 et seq. Defendant's sole contention that there is no proof Plaintiff American Express Centurion is not the "American Express entity" which he concedes "it is clear he owes" is meritless. GRANT plaintiff's request for judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ERNESTO FLORESCA VS. JAMES A MCELROY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT JAMES MCELROY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED with leave to amend. A claim for professional negligence must plead facts showing not only a duty and breach, but also that but for the alleged malpractice, it is more likely than not that the plaintiff would have obtained a more favorable result. (Shopoff & Cavallo LLP v. Hyon (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1489, 1509-11.) Plaintiff asserts that if McElroy had read the sentencing memorandum properly, Plaintiff would have been able to challenge his sentence and restitution fine. He does not plead, however, facts showing that he actually would have obtained a better result in the absence of McElroy's negligence. Sentencing is within the discretion of the trial judge; it is not clear on what basis Plaintiff believes he could have obtained a more lenient sentence absent McElroy's admitted negligence. Plaintiff may amend his complaint to plead facts, if he is able to do so, showing how he would have avoided going to jail or paying the restitution fine but for McElroy's negligence. Defendant's argument that the claim is barred by the statute of limitations is rejected. Plaintiff's claim stems from Defendant's misreading of the government's sentencing memorandum in February 2014. Because his suit was filed in November 2014, it was within the 1 year statute of limitations period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ERNESTO FLORESCA VS. JAMES A MCELROY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ERNESTO FLORESCA'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DENIED. Proof of service establishes the demurrer was sent to Plaintiff. Plaintiff obviously received notice at some point, or he would not have known to oppose the demurrer, or to file this motion to strike. Plaintiff does not plead how or when he did get notice, any prejudice, or otherwise show why the demurrer should be stricken.
